transcript.text: Thank Kirkland, and anyone else on the East Coast, how did the storm, or how is the storm treating you guys?
It's pretty bad.
Yeah?
Yeah, yeah, everything is closed, like, the store was, like, closed, and so my daughter didn't go to daycare today because they were closed as well.
So, yeah, a lot of snow.
A lot
This is my first time shoveling last night.
I've never shoveled snow before.
That was an experience.
I'm down in Virginia, and it's not, I mean, it's bad, but it's not that bad.
It's like five or six inches, but for us, that also still shuts down our entire city.
I was lucky.

Our daycare, we're like, no, we're open if you can make it here, even though I'm in the East Coast of Canada, and it's like, yeah, very, but it's very fluffy snow this time around, because it's so cold out that it's not like heavy, wet snow, which is really like kills your back when you're moving that.
So it's just like fluffy, fun snow.
We have like yesterday, as it was coming down, was almost like silica gel, like just tiny little balls of ice.
Like it's not snow, it's just falling ice for literally all day yesterday.
And so my son is like 19 months and we were outside.
And he, like, laid down to make, like, a snow angel, and then all the hail started hitting him in the face.
He got so upset.
That's the same here in Texas.
In Austin, we got about two or three inches of snow.
It's just solid ice.
Like, there's no snow.
And I did see a lot of the little kids trying to play with it, like, not being able to, like, form a snowball.
They're like, what's going on?
It's snowing in Austin?
Yeah, yeah.
Schools are shut down.
We're prepared for it.
Like, it happens to us.
Like, every few years, we get a big ice storm, and the whole city just freaks out.
Like, grocery stores were empty, you know, like, lines to get gas.
We've had some bad ones, though.
We're not ready when it comes.
Were there any power outages at all this time?
Not in my section, so I don't think so, because we all, yeah, a few years ago, the entire city was without power during the pandemic, you know, and I still have trauma from it.
I even see snow, and I'm like, oh my god, it's gonna be so bad.
But this time, so far, so good, so, it's supposed to warm up today, so schools were canceled today, but by tomorrow, we're supposed to be all good.
I love Austin, it's such a cool city, though.
It's such a good city.
It's an amazing city, yeah, I love it so much, yeah, and barbecue.
Oh, gosh.
Yeah, the food, honestly, goes crazy.
It does, it goes so good.
We're eating good over here.
Sorry, Panzer.
No, it's all right, it's all right.
Sorry, sorry for being late.
It's just been a mess over here.
Winter time brings all kinds of connection stuff when you live in the rurals.
Okay, what do want to talk about today?
Okay, so Time Doctor, checking in.
We're looking at some good tracking so far from everybody, so please be diligent about that this week and going forward.
We're getting some raw data that's interesting.
I don't have any conclusive patterns to share yet because I want to make sure to collect the

David and slice it the right way, but we're certainly seeing some patterns and seeing opportunities to help, and we're already jumping in with some of you, so, you know, we've been die bombing you and being like, hey, how can we help?

Because we can see the patterns, you know, on the bright side of the patterns, the timing hovers around, you know, 10 to 15 minutes, 20 minutes, and then on the dark side, you know, it can be hours and hours.

So, um, we have means, medians and means that we're looking at, but, uh, overall it's showing us, like, hey, there's clearly some of these work streams where people are really on top of them, uh, and then there's others where people need help, so this is great, because this is exactly what we wanted to see, we needed to see, um, so that's good, please keep, um, tracking that, and then remember that if you have any issues, time tracking, or you need, uh, technical support, um, just hit up Nana or myself, and we'll get you.
Last week.

I talked a little bit about quality standards, I want to make sure that we continue to do, you know, continue to kind of lean into that, remember, you know, read articles before delivery, verify links, we know that we had some, the EPD team found some linking issues in one of the linking workflows, and are deploying some fixes there, so keep looking at that.

Um, don't assume that any sort of manual labor, especially link checking or, uh, any systemic issues, don't assume that's just on you, you know, I mean, obviously it is on you to fix it before the client sees it, we talked about that last week, that remains true today, but it does not mean that it, um, it's something that you have to just live with, uh, coming out of Atlas, if there is a systemic issue with Atlas, that is room for improvement, so let us make that improvement, right, so like, don't, don't, don't a, um, don't file that under, I, I guess this is just my life now, you know, uh, make sure that that
We know that Atlas needs improvement, and the only way to know that, of course, is to file a detailed ticket.
So please make sure that you are filing those tickets.
The fact of the matter is that there may already be a fix-in, or it may be you, just being honest, right?
Like it may be you using it improperly, or just, you know, kind of leading the workflow astray.
In either case, though, you don't have to live with that.
You know, you can either get the training and education or update to your process that you need, or Atlas itself or a workflow can be tweaked or updated.
Yes, Gerglin?
Just on the internal links, I want to give an update there.
So a couple of things we found out is since January 12th up until last week, there was a weird bug in the internal links with the SERP API.
So, like, when it was trying to get information on pages to link to, sometimes it just wouldn't get any because of a weird bug that got introduced from trying to fix a different problem.
That's fixed now.
So, number two, we added Ascension.
Essentially a manual 404 check in the workflow to just like ping all the URLs after it adds them.
It used to do it before, but sometimes it would ping the URL and be like, yeah, it's good.
Give that to the LLM and the LLM would just change the URL for no reason.
So now we're checking again after implementation to make that a little bit better, hopefully.
And third is the internal link target site is an input that should be in all of the pipelines that have an internal link step that used to only allow one domain.
So if you tried putting in like augmentcode.com comma augmentcode.com slash guides, that did not work.
And then it just had nothing to verify from.
And so it was just guessing.
Now it supports either one domain or multiple paths.
So if you just want docs.augmentcode.com and augmentcode.com slash blogs, if you just put those as comma separated strings, it should respect multiple paths.
So
So it'll only include internal links from those different paths.
This is all in theory and worked on a handful of tests.
So if that doesn't work as intended, please let us know.
But in theory, those are a few changes that should make things a little bit better.
Hopefully.
Great.
Thank you.
Appreciate that.
Sweet.
And I guess that dovetails or leads into our introducing our EPD support lead for the, for now, Sergei, why don't you introduce yourself?
Yeah.
Hey, I worked at GrowthX like for almost two months.
Some of you already know me, some know.
So my name is Sergei.
I work as an engineer for almost four years.
But before that, I had a career in marketing, content marketing.
So I worked for seven years as a content marketer from copywriter to head of content.
So I kind of know, guys, what you struggle with.
So I will be happy to help you with everything.
you.
you.
From organizational stuff, everything's still the same.
You just create tickets, put it like in linear, and me or Kirkland or Marcus will handle it.
Yeah, but primarily I will be the person who will check all of them out.
Yeah.
Kirkland, do you have anything to add?

Yeah, basically, you know, the truth is, I think we've talked about this for a while, but like, there are like four of us, maybe five, depending on if Nico or Marcus is off on some, you know, project trying to fix something or I'm stuck in something else, trying to handle all of the work for all of you guys, and that's just challenging, and I've been trying to manage and triage all the linear tickets for the last month, and I think we've done a little bit of a better job in the last few weeks of trying to at least communicate, like, hey, we'll get to this, or like, we can't get to that, or whatever, and Sergei's onboarded really well.

He's already helped a bunch of you guys out with different things, and so just to kind of delineate who's doing what on our side, Sergei is going to step up and kind of be the primary person trying to triage along with me.
And so...
If you're filing a ticket, Sergey's probably the first person that's going to get back to you.
And then if he's like, hey, I don't have time for this, then he's going to help pass it off to somebody else.
But just so you guys have, like, a primary face to think about.
In the future, we'll have more people, and it won't just be one person, so bear with us.
There's only a few of us, but we're trying our best.
Sweet.
Thanks, guys.
Yeah, appreciate that.
Just more help on tickets, triaging, seeing the pipeline issues, seeing the issues that come up.

So the – I actually don't remember if I mentioned this to you, Sergey, but you could probably – if you aren't already, I'm almost assuming you are, but the Atlas Learnings channel is more of our peer review channel for us, so you can join that in Slack.
But then, of course, the EPD client support channel is there, and then linear tickets are there.
That's how they will actually see things.
So peer review is fine, but if you're talking amongst yourselves, don't assume that a member of the EPD team is eavesdropping.
Linear is the source of truth.
So make sure that you are filing your tickets and being processed.
So about that, because it also allows you to collate the information that they need to actually action them, because this feels weird isn't enough.
We need to have some specific detail about what's going on.
Sweet.
That's pretty much all I have for this morning.
Pretty straightforward.
Continue last week's admonitions to be that wall between, you know, good work and bad work going out to the client, and hopefully only let the good stuff out.
And then other than that, happy to hear from you about anything you've got going on, any common patterns you're seeing, or blockers that you have, et cetera, and we can talk about those.
Anybody got something happening?
No?
Nobody's lives are amazing?
I don't know if this is worth sharing, but recently we've been having tons of problems with Claude.
Okay.
Thank Thank
A lot, and I think we raised in the tools channel, but still having tons of troubles, giving us a hard time post-processing everything outside of Atlas.
Okay.
Yeah.
Kirkland, can you go ahead and log into Anthropix repo and just fix whatever there are going on there?
I'd try if I could.
Yeah.
Unfortunately, all of those problems are, like, unrelated to us.
It is good to call out, Patricia.
the one thing, like, so there is, there is some, the one thing I can't address there, because we can't control it, unfortunately, right?
It is of the problem of working with dependencies.
When you have dependencies, you get kind of, you know, run over by the issues that dependencies have.
However, we, there is a channel, I believe it's a whole channel, dedicated to cloud outages and service outages for dependent services, isn't it, Kirkland?
Yes.
What is it called?
Yeah.
I'm only flagging that because, yes, we can't fix it.
Like, we can't just, you know, email it and say, fix our personal problems.
But if they have systemic issues, knowing that they have issues, because they will report it.
You know, and generally, they'll have service outages, issues, or degradations in service reporting.
Having some visibility there can help you to, like, sanity check yourself, because you're like, what is that?
Is it just me?
And you're like, oh, no, it's not me, right?
And in those cases, the action for us to take is obviously to move on to other things until we, you know, until the service stabilizes, and then run your tasks.
Yeah.
Go ahead.
Sorry.
Marcus, do you remember what that channel's called?
My mind is completely blanking.
Whoa.
Whoa.
Or where?
Because I.
know we have like a bot that sends Claude, is it just in the alerts?
Yeah, in the alerts, but it's a very nice channel right now, so maybe we should move these alerts to learning Atlas.
The Claude ones, yeah.
Yeah, somewhere that people see, because, yeah.

Yeah, and I don't think your instincts are wrong there that, in the end, those affordances probably need to be in the UI of Atlas, where it's like, you know, somebody's using Ask.ai as an example, it could report like, hey, our services are, you know, have degraded responses at the moment, or whatever.
I'm not saying that that's urgent.

I think the near-term fix is like a Slack channel where people can kind of see what's going on, or major service outages or degradations in service just get popped into learning Atlas, that's fine too.
I'm just going to make a new channel called IsClawedDown, and I'll just link the alerts to that.
There you go.
Sweet.
Sweet.
Sweet.
Sweet.
Sweet.
Sweet.
Also, if somehow there's any, because using Cloud, the website, we are using their chat app.

If there's another separate app that can do everything everyone's doing with Cloud, we can try to use a different provider of Cloud models, as Google, Google offers Cloud, and you have an account with Cloud.
So, if suddenly, we can try to find a different chat app that's able to do what you are doing, but we use a different provider as Google-provided Cloud.
So, it's a price-level solution for the problem.
Right, right, right, right.
Yeah, that's worth, it's worth basically clarifying on that a little bit, I think, because I would guess that there's a good chunk of people on this call that don't understand that.
There is, so, like, when you say, like, Claude is being whatever, there are a couple of ways that that can present itself.
So, like, if Claude is having issues, and those issues are systemic to, like, model delivery from the API, that will affect Atlas directly.
Because Atlas pulls the API and says, like, you know, uses it to chew on the problem and then returns results.

So, the Ask.ai tool, um, your, many of the pipelines now use, like, Opus, uh, some of them use previous versions still because they're consistent for that, for that, um, outcome that they're desired there.
But, when you are talking about Claude being whatever, and you are referring to opening Claude.ai and chatting with it and saying, like, hey, I'd like you to do this for me.
And, can you crunch these, this article, and can you run this post-process in a Claude project, etc.?
There are other ways to access Claude that may not be seeing difficulties at the moment.
Because Anthropic is providing that service directly, whereas Google also serves Anthropic models.
So you can use, like, Gemini, but call Anthrop, or like, Google's chat interface, but call Opus, and then perform the same task.
The problem there, of course, is that you don't have, like, your projects set up there, right?
If your projects are set up in the Cloud Chat interface, et cetera.

I just don't feel like it's systemic enough at this point, because, really, like, the best providers and the providers of the most cutting-edge versions of those are almost always going to be Anthropic.
And then, you know, you're sort of, like, cascading downwards in efficacy and whether they're serving the latest models and how they're serving them, et cetera.
If you're going, like, okay, cool, I'm going to bounce over to Google and whatever.
But it can be a great fallback if you're, like, in a real bind, trying to deliver and trying to do some post-processing.
Set yourself up in Gemini and then call Opus 4.5, and you're going to get very...

similar results, probably, to calling Opus 4.5 via Claude, and this is an option in many cases, it's not just Google, by the way, there are other providers that also serve Anthropic models, if you want a Galaxy brain in it, you can just build an interface for yourself that calls whatever you want, and it's very, very, it's actually, it'll take you afternoon, but I'm not saying, I'm not, this is not an official mandate, or an official recommendation to anybody, call, just saying, if you want a noodle, you know, my Claude bot has access to whatever models I choose, some of those are local, and so if something's giving me fits, or if I want to work on something locally, where I don't want to pass it to an LLM, you can have it run local, these things are, are very achievable now, if you want to do that, but for production work, the official recommendation is, of course, use your standard Claude projects, if you're really running up against something, and you're wondering how to do that, you reach out in Atlas Learning, hey, does anybody
I'm a success for themselves where I'm having a heck of a time and then we can go from there.
Yeah, because the API and cloud code has been stable, so just the cloud AI that has been failing that often, so, yeah.
Yeah, I'm not sure what's going on.
We try to start conversations and straight up, it's not compressing or whatever, it gives an error and we just can't continue.
So, that's been going on with other people, as well, and we can't really figure out.

Yeah, I mean, the thing that Hassan was mentioning, I will call out, it is important to understand that, like, the compressor has limits, and generally speaking, if it's regularly compressing in your conversations, it's probably because you're feeding it to, like, enormous context and expecting it to just chew through it.
So, it might be better to do your work in more manageable chunks, like, per conversation, but, you know.
It's, the theory is that it should be able to compress that conversation and carry the context forward, that's the reason that the compression task exists.

That came from Cloud Code, by the way, so that was like a Cloud Code invention, so to speak, and not, they didn't invent it, the concept was invented academically, but they implemented it for Cloud Code, and then that cascaded to the Cloud.ai chat.

So that's, that's something that came from the fact that, like, developers are like, man, I wish I could continue this context of this, you know, million word database or code base that I want to, like, continue working on, and, and you're now, you know, you're now telling me I got to start over.
And so that came from that world.
But the fact, it doesn't do anything super magical, it just basically takes all of the data that's there, and then creates a light database of it, so that it can reference it going forward.
And if you are feeding enormous amounts of information, it can kind of fall on its face a little bit, so.
Just flagging that.
Now, I have been experimenting with and doing more work on my articles and content that I use in CloudCode because it tends to work pretty well as well.
I use it in Cursor.
So you could also experiment with that if you're feeling spicy.
But overall, the chat interface should work pretty well, and only in the past couple of weeks has it gotten really rocky, and I'm assuming that's just because of increased demand.
So maybe write it out, maybe try to use an alternate interface for the chat, or maybe try to, you know, tap somebody else for assistance and see if they can help you rank what you need.
Thank you.
Yeah, yeah.
Any other questions, comments, issues?
Okay.
Sweet.
Good.
Let's go do work.
It's interesting having this work on Monday, or this on a Monday, because I have less to talk about, but by next Monday, I'll have seven days, and then I'll have more to talk about.
But this Monday, you're getting a little bit of a pass, because I haven't seen all the week's problems yet.
So we'll see how that goes.
But thanks, folks.
Appreciate it.
Have a good one.
Ciao, ciao.
Yeah, bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review production workflows, quality standards, and EPD support.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=279.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Time Doctor data shows clear workflow bottlenecks,** with task times varying from 10–20 minutes to several hours. The EPD team is using this data to proactively offer support.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=281.0)
  - [**Sergey Kaplich is now the primary EPD support lead,** triaging all Linear tickets to streamline the process.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
  - [**Critical internal linking bugs are fixed,** including a SERP API issue and a new post-implementation 404 check. The `internal_link_target_site` input now supports multiple paths.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
  - **Claude.ai chat is unstable,** but the API is stable. A new Slack channel, `#is-claude-down`, will track outages, and Google's Gemini is a viable fallback for post-processing.

## Topics

### Time Doctor Insights

  - [Initial data shows clear workflow bottlenecks, with task times varying from 10–20 minutes to several hours.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=281.0)
  - [The EPD team is using this data to proactively offer support where needed.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
  - [**Action:** Continue diligent time tracking. Contact Nana or Matthew for support.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=281.0)

### Quality Standards & Systemic Issues

  - [**Directive:** Maintain quality standards (e.g., read articles, verify links) to act as the final quality gate before client delivery.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [**Action:** File detailed Linear tickets for any systemic issues in Atlas.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This provides the EPD team with the specific data needed to diagnose and fix problems, preventing the team from having to "live with" broken workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)

### Internal Linking Updates

  - [Kirkland detailed three key fixes to improve internal linking accuracy:](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
    1.  [**SERP API Bug Fix:** Resolved an issue (Jan 12–last week) that prevented the API from fetching page data for linking.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
    2.  **Post-Implementation 404 Check:** Added a check to ping all URLs *after* the LLM implements them.
          - [**Rationale:** Fixes an issue where the LLM would sometimes change valid URLs, causing 404s.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
    3.  **Multiple Path Support:** The `internal_link_target_site` input now accepts comma-separated paths (e.g., `domain.com/blogs,domain.com/docs`).
          - [**Rationale:** Fixes an issue where the system would guess links if multiple paths were provided.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=501.0)

### EPD Support & Ticket Triage

  - [Sergey Kaplich is now the primary EPD support lead, triaging all Linear tickets.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This streamlines support by providing a single point of contact and better managing the EPD team's workload.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=617.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
      - [File all issues in Linear; it is the source of truth for tracking and resolution.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
      - Use the `#atlas-learnings` Slack channel for peer review and discussion.

### Claude.ai Instability & Workarounds

  - [**Problem:** The Claude.ai chat interface is experiencing frequent errors.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=773.0)
  - [**Diagnosis:** The issue is with the chat interface, not the Claude API, which remains stable.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=1206.0)
      - [**Note:** The "compressing" error often occurs when feeding the model excessively large contexts.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=1230.0)
  - [**Workarounds:**](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=829.0)
      - **Outage Tracking:** A new Slack channel, `#is-claude-down`, will track service outages.
      - [**Fallback Interface:** Use Google's Gemini to call Claude models (e.g., Opus).](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=1125.0)
          - [**Rationale:** This provides a stable alternative for post-processing tasks.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=1125.0)
      - [**Custom Interface (Advanced):** Build a personal interface to call any model, including local ones.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**All:** Continue diligent time tracking in Time Doctor.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=281.0)
  - [**All:** File detailed Linear tickets for all systemic Atlas issues.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=381.0)
  - [**All:** Use Google's Gemini as a fallback for Claude post-processing during outages.](https://fathom.video/share/vbLbnhUsycUNCTWqU4D1ohzzN5q-DmLC?tab=summary&timestamp=1125.0)
  - **Kirkland:** Create the `#is-claude-down` Slack channel and link Claude service alerts to it.

action_items.description: Investigate Anthropic repo issues; fix if possible
Create Slack channel IsClawedDown; route Claude outage alerts
